profile.name: ComplianceGuard Operational AI RMF Profile
profile.description: Cross-sectoral profile for LLM and data-pipeline AI use: GAIRA governance, model registry, dataset scanning, execution validation, and runtime guard.

controls.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
controls.text: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.
controls.notes: GAIRA EU AI Act module supports legal applicability; formal legal register is organizational.

controls.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
controls.text: The characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into organizational policies, processes, procedures, and practices.
controls.notes: Atomic rules and policy bundles encode privacy/security trustworthiness checks.

controls.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
controls.text: Processes are in place to determine the needed level of risk management activities based on organizational risk tolerance.
controls.notes: GAIRA risk levels and scoring thresholds; org risk appetite is manual config.

controls.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
controls.text: The risk management process and its outcomes are established through transparent policies, procedures, and other controls.
controls.notes: Active policies, enabled rules, and audit trail provide operational controls.

controls.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
controls.text: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the risk management process and its outcomes are planned and roles defined.
controls.notes: RBAC roles, audit logs, and gap analysis runs support periodic review.

controls.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
controls.text: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems and are resourced according to organizational risk priorities.
controls.notes: ROAIA (AI applications) and model registry provide inventory.

controls.category_title: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
controls.text: Processes are in place for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely.
controls.notes: Not automated; requires org lifecycle process.

controls.category_title: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
controls.text: Roles and responsibilities related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks are documented and clear.
controls.notes: RBAC permissions map to Admin/User/Auditor responsibilities.

controls.category_title: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
controls.text: Personnel and partners receive AI risk management training consistent with policies and procedures.
controls.notes: Training is organizational; not tracked in platform.

controls.category_title: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
controls.text: Executive leadership takes responsibility for decisions about AI system development and deployment risks.
controls.notes: GAIRA submit records proceed/no-go decisions; executive attestation is manual.

controls.category_title: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.
controls.text: Decision-making is informed by a diverse team (demographics, disciplines, experience, expertise, backgrounds).
controls.notes: DEI and human-AI oversight processes are organizational.

controls.category_title: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.
controls.text: Policies define roles and responsibilities for human-AI configurations and oversight of AI systems.
controls.notes: DEI and human-AI oversight processes are organizational.

controls.category_title: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
controls.text: Policies foster a critical thinking and safety-first mindset in AI design, development, deployment, and use.
controls.notes: Block/warn policies and guard enforce safety-first execution.

controls.category_title: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
controls.text: Teams document risks and potential impacts of AI technology and communicate about impacts.
controls.notes: GAIRA assessments and scan/report artifacts document impacts.

controls.category_title: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.
controls.text: Practices enable AI testing, identification of incidents, and information sharing.
controls.notes: Threat detection, guard violations, and audit logs support incident identification.

controls.category_title: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.
controls.text: Policies collect, consider, prioritize, and integrate feedback from external parties on AI risks and impacts.
controls.notes: GAIRA 2nd-line review for flagged answers; broader engagement is manual.

controls.category_title: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.
controls.text: Mechanisms incorporate adjudicated feedback from relevant AI actors into system design and implementation.
controls.notes: GAIRA 2nd-line review for flagged answers; broader engagement is manual.

controls.category_title: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.
controls.text: Policies address AI risks associated with third-party entities including IP infringement.
controls.notes: Model registry approval workflow for external/cloud models.

controls.category_title: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.
controls.text: Contingency processes handle failures or incidents in third-party data or high-risk AI systems.
controls.notes: Pre-execution validation and guard block/warn on risky third-party use.

controls.category_title: Context is established and understood.
controls.text: Intended purposes, context-specific laws, norms, deployment settings are documented.

controls.category_title: Context is established and understood.
controls.text: Interdisciplinary diverse AI actors for establishing context are documented.
controls.notes: Organizational process not tracked in platform.

controls.category_title: Context is established and understood.
controls.text: Organization mission and relevant AI goals are understood and documented.

controls.category_title: Context is established and understood.
controls.text: Business value or context of business use is clearly defined or re-evaluated.

controls.category_title: Context is established and understood.
controls.text: Organizational risk tolerances are determined and documented.

controls.category_title: Context is established and understood.
controls.text: System requirements (e.g., privacy of users) are elicited and understood.

controls.category_title: Categorization of the AI system is performed.
controls.text: Tasks and methods the AI system will support are defined.

controls.category_title: Categorization of the AI system is performed.
controls.text: Knowledge limits and human oversight of system output are documented.

controls.category_title: Categorization of the AI system is performed.
controls.text: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations are identified and documented.

controls.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
controls.text: Potential benefits of intended AI functionality are examined and documented.

controls.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
controls.text: Potential costs from AI errors or trustworthiness issues are examined and documented.

controls.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
controls.text: Targeted application scope is specified based on capability and context.

controls.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
controls.text: Operator proficiency processes with AI performance and trustworthiness are defined.
controls.notes: Organizational process not tracked in platform.

controls.category_title: AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs compared with appropriate benchmarks are understood.
controls.text: Human oversight processes are defined per GOVERN policies.

controls.category_title: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system including third-party software and data.
controls.text: Approaches for mapping third-party component risks are in place and documented.

controls.category_title: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system including third-party software and data.
controls.text: Internal risk controls for AI components including third-party technologies are documented.

controls.category_title: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society are characterized.
controls.text: Likelihood and magnitude of identified impacts are documented.

controls.category_title: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society are characterized.
controls.text: Practices for engaging AI actors and integrating feedback on impacts are in place.

controls.category_title: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
controls.text: Approaches and metrics for MAP risks are selected; unmeasurable risks documented.

controls.category_title: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
controls.text: Appropriateness of AI metrics and control effectiveness are regularly assessed.

controls.category_title: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.
controls.text: Independent assessors and domain experts are involved in regular assessments.
controls.notes: Independent TEVV not automated in platform.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: AI system functionality and behavior are monitored in production.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: AI system security and resilience are evaluated and documented.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: Transparency and accountability risks are examined and documented.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: Privacy risk of the AI system is examined and documented.

controls.category_title: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.
controls.text: Approaches track existing, unanticipated, and emergent AI risks over time.

controls.category_title: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.
controls.text: Feedback processes for end users to report problems are established.
controls.notes: User appeal workflows not yet in platform.

controls.category_title: Feedback about efficacy of measurement is gathered and assessed.
controls.text: Measurement approaches are connected to deployment contexts and documented.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: Test sets, metrics, and TEVV tools are documented.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: Evaluations involving human subjects meet applicable requirements.
controls.notes: Human subjects research not in scope.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: Performance or assurance criteria are measured for deployment-like conditions.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: AI system is demonstrated valid and reliable; generalization limits documented.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: AI system is evaluated for safety risks with fail-safe behavior.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: AI model is explained, validated, and output interpreted in context.
controls.notes: Explainability not implemented.

controls.category_title: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
controls.text: Fairness and bias are evaluated and documented.
controls.notes: Bias evaluation not implemented.

controls.text: Environmental impact of AI training and management is assessed.

controls.text: Effectiveness of TEVV metrics and processes is evaluated.

controls.text: Risk tracking for hard-to-measure settings is considered.

controls.text: Measurement results are validated with domain expert input.

controls.category_title: Feedback about efficacy of measurement is gathered and assessed.
controls.text: Performance improvements or declines from field data are documented.

controls.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
controls.text: Determination whether AI system should proceed based on intended purposes.

controls.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
controls.text: Treatment of documented AI risks is prioritized by impact and likelihood.

controls.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
controls.text: Responses to high-priority MAP risks are developed, planned, and documented.

controls.category_title: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.
controls.text: Negative residual risks to acquirers and end users are documented.

controls.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
controls.text: Resources to manage AI risks and non-AI alternatives are considered.
controls.notes: Resource planning is organizational.

controls.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
controls.text: Mechanisms sustain value of deployed AI systems.

controls.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
controls.text: Procedures respond to and recover from previously unknown risks.

controls.category_title: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.
controls.text: Mechanisms supersede or deactivate AI systems with inconsistent outcomes.

controls.category_title: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.
controls.text: Third-party AI risks and benefits are monitored with controls applied.

controls.category_title: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.
controls.text: Pre-trained models are monitored as part of regular maintenance.

controls.category_title: Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.
controls.text: Post-deployment monitoring plans include incident response and decommissioning.

controls.category_title: Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.
controls.text: Continual improvement activities are integrated into AI system updates.

controls.category_title: Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.
controls.text: Incidents and errors are communicated; tracking and recovery are documented.
